title: scenario_phishing_email
description: A mass phishing email campaign targets your organisation, attempting to harvest credentials.

timeline.title: Suspicious Emails Arrive
timeline.description: Staff report emails impersonating the IT helpdesk asking them to verify login credentials via a link.

timeline.title: First Victims Click
timeline.description: Two employees have already clicked the link and entered their passwords on a fake login page.

timeline.title: Accounts Accessed
timeline.description: Stolen credentials used to access internal systems within minutes of being submitted.

timeline.title: Lateral Movement Detected
timeline.description: Attacker uses compromised accounts to search shared drives and email accounts for sensitive data.

timeline.title: Containment Actions
timeline.description: IT locks compromised accounts and pushes an organisation-wide warning to ignore the emails.

timeline.title: Post-Incident Review
timeline.description: Analyse how the phishing email bypassed filters and update email security rules.

node-1.title: Phishing Email Reported
node-1.situation: Multiple staff members have forwarded a suspicious email claiming to be from the IT helpdesk. The email asks recipients to click a link and confirm their login credentials due to a 'security upgrade'. Some employees say they have already clicked the link.
node-1.question: What is your immediate first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Send an urgent all-staff alert warning not to click the link, and report the email to the IT security team for analysis.

options.label: B
options.text: Click the link yourself to verify whether it is truly malicious before deciding what to do.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait for IT to notice on their own — it is their responsibility to handle suspicious emails.

node-2.title: Employees Already Compromised
node-2.situation: IT confirms two employees entered their credentials on the fake site. Their accounts may now be under attacker control.
node-2.question: How do you handle the compromised accounts?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately disable the compromised accounts and force password resets with MFA enrolment before re-enabling them.

options.label: B
options.text: Ask the affected employees to change their passwords themselves at their own convenience.

options.label: C
options.text: Leave the accounts active to monitor attacker activity without notifying the employees.

node-3.title: Email Filter Bypass
node-3.situation: Analysis reveals the phishing email bypassed the spam filter by using a legitimate cloud service as a redirect. The same technique could be used again.
node-3.question: What technical control do you implement?

options.label: A
options.text: Update email security rules to flag external links using redirect services, and enable advanced anti-phishing policies.

options.label: B
options.text: Block all external email links entirely, preventing employees from clicking any external URL.

node-4.title: Scope of Data Access
node-4.situation: Forensics shows the attacker used the compromised accounts to access shared drives containing HR records and project files for approximately 20 minutes.
node-4.question: What is your next step regarding the data exposure?

options.label: A
options.text: Conduct a data impact assessment, notify affected individuals per data protection obligations, and document the breach.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep the breach internal to avoid reputational damage — the access window was short.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Compromised accounts have been secured, the phishing campaign has been contained, affected individuals notified, and email controls updated. A follow-up security awareness session has been scheduled for all staff.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
